data.title: Intent-contradicting gate auto-closes feature requests instead of escalating to user
data.description:
## What happened

During the 2026-04-02 debug-everything session, bug 32f8-59c1 (TEST_FILTER env var not supported) was auto-closed as intent-contradicting because the feature was never implemented. Gate 1a correctly identified that the reported behavior matched system intent (feature never existed), but the workflow then auto-closed the ticket without user input.

## Expected behavior

When Gate 1a returns intent-contradicting and the bug is actually a feature request (not a misunderstanding of existing behavior), the workflow should escalate to the user rather than auto-closing. The user should decide whether to:
1. Close as not-a-bug
2. Convert to a feature epic via /dso:brainstorm
3. Keep open for future consideration

## Root cause

fix-bug SKILL.md Step 1.5 (Gate 1a) treats all intent-contradicting results the same: auto-close with reason. It does not distinguish between 'working as designed' (correct to auto-close) and 'feature never existed' (should escalate — the user may want the feature).

## Affected file

plugins/dso/skills/fix-bug/SKILL.md — Step 1.5 intent-contradicting handling (lines 224-233)
